Heavy Metal Poisoning

Heavy metals are inorganic compounds that the body is not capable of metabolizing. Unfortunately, the increase in pollution of all kinds, in all types of environments, toxic waste, etc. means that humans are exposed to the ingestion, absorption, and exposure of these metals which become toxins that the body cannot eliminate. They accumulate in organs and tissues, and if not with the help of "chelators" (substances that trap the inorganic molecules of heavy metals and create compounds that the body *can* eliminate through urine), they could not be removed.

There are different substances that "chelate" heavy metals. Despite this, the presence of heavy metals in tissues creates an electromagnetic impregnation. Even if physical elimination is successful, this impregnation remains, causing the same symptoms as if the heavy metals were still present, seemingly eliminated.

Only through the elimination of these frequencies via Bio-resonance Mora is complete elimination possible, as well as the restoration of the function of the affected tissue.

List of Most Common Heavy Metals and Where They Are Found

ALUMINUM:
– Accumulates in lungs and brain. Blocks magnesium absorption, causes fatigue, and contributes to the onset of Alzheimer's.
– Found in non-organic vegetables, industrial food, tap water, deodorants, amalgams, vaccines, pain relievers, and antacids.

ARSENIC
– Accumulates in liver, kidneys, skin, and nervous system.
– Inhibits the action of vitamin E, selenium, and sulfur. Causes chronic fatigue, lung and bladder cancer, diabetes, and gastrointestinal problems. found in tap water, packaged juices, whole grains (especially rice), and wines.
To remove arsenic from rice, rinse it several times and boil it in plenty of water (about 6 times its volume).

CADMIUM
– Accumulates in the respiratory tract, liver, kidneys, fat, nerves, and bones.
– Blocks the absorption of zinc, magnesium, selenium, and sulfur.
– Causes hypertension, headache, anemia, peripheral nervous system disorders, kidney and lung problems.
– Found in tap water, freshwater fish, crustaceans, leafy vegetables, mushrooms, and non-organic dried seaweed.

MERCURY
– Accumulates in kidneys, brain, and nervous system.
– Prevents the absorption of zinc, selenium, and iron. Destroys vitamin B12 and causes chronic fatigue, high blood pressure, infections, anemia, and immune problems.
– Found in large fish, intensive agriculture pesticides, vaccines, and dental amalgams.

LEAD
– Accumulates in the brain, nervous system, and bones. Blocks iron, calcium, and molybdenum absorption. Causes musculoskeletal pain, irritability, sleep disorders, and cognitive changes.
– Found in water channeled in lead pipes, pesticides, packaged products, medications, tobacco smoke.

ALGAE AND MUSHROOMS: BEWARE! If they come from contaminated places, they constitute authentic heavy metal bombs.

The erasure of metal frequencies via bio-resonance M. guarantees their complete elimination.

See the procedure here.

“Before curing anyone, ask him if he is willing to give up the things that made him sick.”

— Hippocrates

“Health depends on being in harmony with our souls.”

— Edward Bach

“Medicine must integrate body, soul and spirit so that the treatment is true and lasting.”

— Rudolf Steiner

“The true doctor not only prescribes, but listens, understands and guides towards balance.”

— Anonymous

“Homeopathy does not treat diseases, but people who are sick.”

— André Saine

“The body whispers before it screams; the attentive doctor listens to the silence of the disease before it manifests.”

— Proposed Quote

“Technology heals, chemistry acts, but the soul is cured in the presence of love and listening.”

— Proposed Quote

“The physician's high and only mission is to restore the sick to health, to cure, as it is termed.”

— Samuel Hahnemann

“The good physician treats the disease; the great physician treats the patient who has the disease.”

— William Osler
